Table of Contents

Blazor

DrawnUI for Blazor now has three package identities and two runtime models:

  • DrawnUi.Blazor.Wasm for browser-side / WebAssembly rendering
  • DrawnUi.Blazor.Server for server-rendered DrawnUI surfaces inside Blazor Server and InteractiveServer apps
  • DrawnUi.Blazor.Core is a shared foundation, you don't have to install it manually

This page is the entry point for Blazor consumers and the base for the dedicated runtime docs.

Start Here

  • Blazor Packages for package roles, install targets, and reference layout
  • Blazor WebAssembly for browser-side rendering with Canvas
  • Blazor Server for server-rendered DrawnUI with ServerCanvas
  • Blazor Hybrid Web App for mixed InteractiveServer and InteractiveWebAssembly apps
  • Blazor Capabilities for current strengths, limitations, and runtime fit
  • Blazor Migration for adoption paths from existing Blazor apps
  • Blazor FAQ for package choice, support limits, and common adoption questions

Runtime Summary

Runtime Primary package Rendering happens Best fit
Browser / WebAssembly DrawnUi.Blazor.Wasm In the browser Animation-heavy, gesture-heavy, or fully drawn pages
Blazor Server DrawnUi.Blazor.Server On the server, delivered as image frames Event-driven widgets, dashboards, mixed Razor + DrawnUI pages
Mixed Blazor Web App DrawnUi.Blazor.Server in host, DrawnUi.Blazor.Wasm in .Client Per component boundary Apps that need one DrawnUI surface to stay local and another to stay server-owned

Current Scope

Validated today:

  • UseDrawnUiAsync() startup hook for the browser/WASM runtime
  • shared DrawnUiStartupSettings
  • Canvas hosting in Razor pages
  • ServerCanvas hosting for server-rendered DrawnUI frames
  • static-web-asset font loading through DrawnExtensions.RegisterFont(...)
  • KeyboardManager integration for the browser runtime
  • mixed-mode sample with sibling server and WASM DrawnUI panels on the same page

Still true today:

  • Blazor support is in progress, not full MAUI parity
  • validated slices are the safe reference point
  • the port status board remains the source of truth for broader parity progress

See the current implementation status here:

  • Shared / MAUI / Blazor Port Status

Working Samples

  • src/Blazor/Samples/BlazorSandbox/ for browser/WASM DrawnUI
  • src/Blazor/Samples/BlazorSandboxServer/ for Blazor Server ServerCanvas
  • src/Blazor/Samples/BlazorSandboxHybrid/ for mixed server + WASM DrawnUI in one app
